commit.message:
fix: implement full remediation plan across runtime, persistence, sync, and async (#8)

* chore: minor tweaks

* fix: implement full remediation plan across runtime, persistence, sync, and async

Implements all 20 findings from the code quality remediation plan:

Phase 1 - Critical:
- F-01: Add Svelte store contract (subscribe/set) directly on StoreShell
- F-02: Guard getDefaultStateManager() against SSR with runtime error
- F-03: SSR-safe storage adapters with deferred resolution and no-op fallback
- F-04: Validate remote sync state with filterToKnownKeys before patching
- F-05/F-06: Cap devtools timeline with maxEntries (default 500)

Phase 2 - Error Handling and Security:
- F-08: Diagnostic deserialization with DeserializeResult discriminated union
- F-07: Catch QuotaExceededError in storage adapters
- F-10: Wrap sync transport subscribe/publish in try-catch
- F-14: Catch compression/decompression errors with fallback
- F-11: Use addEventListener/removeEventListener for BroadcastChannel

Phase 3 - Consistency and Performance:
- F-09/F-13: Remove registeredDefinitionIds; duplicate detection in StateManager
- F-20: Memoize option store getters with $derived.by()
- F-18: Add subpath exports for persistence, history, sync, async

Phase 4 - Tests and CI:
- Store contract interop, SSR safety, and deserialization tests
- Sync conflict resolution and stale mutation rejection tests
- Storage quota error and corrupted data rehydration tests
- Fix orphaned test in track-async-action.spec.ts
- Fix docs.yml GitHub Pages environment name
- Add vitest coverage thresholds (v8, 70%)
- Fix markdown hard tab lint errors

All validation passes: check (0 errors), lint (clean), test (63/63), build (ok).

* chore: update release workflow, svgs, and vite config

* chore: fix md formatting

* fix: address review feedback — Set prototype safety, subscribe invalidate callback

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/selfagency/stately/sessions/dfa667a4-7d02-4d1a-8349-ab78807b0a02

commit.message:
Add Apple Silicon (MPS) support for fine-tuning

- Detect CUDA / MPS / CPU with _get_device()
- Skip 4-bit quantization on MPS (bitsandbytes CUDA-only)
- Load model in float16 directly on MPS
- Use bf16=True training on MPS, fp16=True on CUDA
- Remove CUDA-only hard error in main()

128GB Apple Silicon Mac can fine-tune Llama 3 8B in float16 (~16GB).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove .value from shallowRef in template (auto-unwrapped by Vue)

shallowRef is a top-level ref in <script setup>, so Vue auto-unwraps it
in the template. Writing imageOptions.value in the template accesses a
non-existent property on the inner object → undefined → 500 error.

- Template: imageOptions.value.cropEnabled → imageOptions.cropEnabled
- Template: imageOptions.value.crop → imageOptions.crop
- Template: :options="imageOptions.value" → :options="imageOptions"
- Script functions (onExport, onGenerateGif) retain .value correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement SQLite storage backend (#5)

* Implement SQLite backend for celer-mem

Add SQLiteBackend class satisfying the StorageBackend concept, with:
- WAL journal mode for better concurrency
- Busy timeout configuration
- RAII lifecycle management
- Full key-value operations: get, put, del, prefix_scan, batch, compact, foreach_scan
- One SQLite db file per table (same isolation model as RocksDB backend)
- 13 dedicated tests covering all operations, persistence, 10K records, concurrency

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/f02ff92c-c356-4d2f-a0d0-bba284d27e7e

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: SQLite backend uses real SQL tables per scope instead of separate db files

Each logical table is now a real SQL table inside a shared scope-level
.db file (e.g. project.db contains "tasks" and "notes" tables), rather
than creating separate .db files per table with a generic "kv" table.

This properly mirrors how RocksDB column families partition a single DB:
- One sqlite3 connection per scope (shared via shared_ptr)
- Each logical table → CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS "<table>" (...)
- batch() uses SAVEPOINT for safe nesting on shared connections
- Factory caches scope connections in an internal map
- Makefile auto-detects sqlite3 like it does rocksdb

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/14e50cde-4434-4ecc-8315-de02942e1a80

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

* security: harden SQLite backend against SQL injection via table/scope names

Add validate_ident() that enforces a strict [a-zA-Z0-9_] allowlist for
all table and scope names before they reach any SQL. Also harden
quote_ident() to strip NUL bytes as defense-in-depth. Add test covering
injection attempts (semicolons, NUL bytes, quotes, path traversal).

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Solesius/celer-mem/sessions/0b7b3406-7f0e-4272-a486-1f8eeb249b2a

Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Solesius <38218017+Solesius@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement freeform CSG subtract/merge with explicit geometry, crash fix, and cutter visual differentiation

- Add BrushGeometry with explicit faces/vertices for boolean result brushes
- Implement BSP-based BrushBooleanKernel for subtract and merge operations
- Subtract: selected cutter carves all intersecting brushes, cutter consumed
- Merge: selected brushes combined into single result brush
- Tag cutter-sourced faces (IsCutterFace) for visual differentiation
- Render cutter inner-wall wireframes at 30% alpha in all viewports
- Fix GL thread-safety crash: move buffer pruning into renderer Render() methods
- Add BrushBounds and hit-testing support for explicit geometry brushes
- Persist explicit geometry in map format with backward compatibility
- Add compact toolbar layout with grouped primitive and boolean actions
- Add dark toolbar button template to prevent white styling artifacts
- 82 tests passing (28 core, 12 formats, 28 app, 14 rendering)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: cover --global branches in list and install commands

Add two integration tests to cli_tests.rs that exercise the previously
uncovered True branches at main.rs:979 (install --global) and main.rs:997
(list --global):

- list_global_no_installed_plugins: runs `aipm list --global` with an
  isolated HOME directory (no installed.json), verifying the empty-registry
  path prints 'No globally installed plugins.'
- install_global_local_plugin: runs `aipm install --global local:./my-plugin`
  with an isolated HOME, verifying success output and that installed.json
  is written with the spec.

Branch coverage: 93.50% → 93.74% (TOTAL), main.rs: 65.31% → 70.41%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Tag cutter-sourced faces and visually differentiate inner walls

After boolean subtract, the replacement brush's inner-wall faces
(cutter-sourced polygons from BSP CSG) were visually indistinguishable
from the original cutter outline. This confused users into thinking the
cutter was still rendered.

Changes:
- Add IsCutterFace runtime flag to BrushFace (not serialized)
- Tag cutter-sourced faces in BrushBooleanKernel.BuildResult()
- Propagate flag through MeshSurfaceRange and MeshGenerator
- Split wireframe edge indices into target/cutter in MeshEdgeIndexBuilder
- Orthographic renderer: draw cutter edges at 30% alpha
- Perspective renderer: enable backface culling for cutter surfaces,
  dim cutter wireframe edges at 30% alpha
- Add unit tests for cutter ID removal and IsCutterFace tagging
- Add acceptance test verifying cutter absent from render diagnostics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add permission approval toggle

Settings → Features → 'Auto-approve Permissions' toggle (ON by default).

When OFF, file writes, shell commands, MCP calls, and other
permission requests show an inline Allow/Deny prompt in the
terminal instead of being auto-approved.

Implementation:
- Bridge: configurable onPermissionRequest handler with pending
  promise map (same pattern as user_input)
- Server: forwards permission_request events, handles responses,
  set_auto_approve message syncs toggle state
- Frontend: inline permission prompt with Allow/Deny buttons,
  Settings toggle persisted to localStorage
- Uses SDK's 'denied-interactively-by-user' result kind for denials

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(011): wizard bug fixes — back-nav, resume, cursor, labels, summary, docs (#114)

Refs: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94, #66, #103

* docs(plan): add implementation plan for 011-wizard-bug-fixes

- Fill plan.md with full Phase 0 research and Phase 1 design
- Add research.md: 7 research findings (focus detection, cursor restore,
  resume exit guard, history pre-population, dimColor, summary gaps, docs)
- Add data-model.md: StepNavProps changes, extractStepValues mapping,
  ConfigSummary additions, cursor restore pattern, resume state guard
- Add quickstart.md: per-bug implementation checklist with commit order
- Add contracts/wizard-ux.md: back-nav, resume, cursor restore UX contracts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(tasks): generate task breakdown for 011-wizard-bug-fixes

27 tasks across 10 phases covering 7 user stories:
- US1 (P1): focus-aware back nav — StepNav + wizard + contexts + app-config (T003–T008)
- US2 (P1): resume exit guard + history pre-population (T009–T012)
- US3 (P1): cursor restore via process signal handlers (T013–T014)
- US4 (P2): remove dimColor from optional step (opt) label (T015)
- US5 (P2): add browser + aiTools to config summary (T016–T017)
- US6 (P2): contexts language/version multi-select + validation (T018–T021)
- US7 (P2): update getting-started.md + config-reference.md for spec 010 (T022–T023)

Refs: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94, #66, #103

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): resolve back-nav, resume, cursor, label, summary, and version validation bugs

- StepNav: add isInputFocused + onAtFirstStep props; guard useInput when input focused
- wizard: add extractStepValues() for resume history pre-population; clamp resume step to LAST_STEP; show first-step b-key hint; remove dimColor from (opt) sidebar label
- config-summary: add Browser and AI Coding Tools conditional sections
- index: restore terminal cursor on exit, SIGINT (130), SIGTERM (143)
- contexts: versionError state + inline validation for lang-version-manual phase
- tests: +25 tests covering StepNav, extractStepValues, resume clamping, ConfigSummary, LanguageBindingSchema
- docs: update getting-started.md to 13-step STEP_REGISTRY; update config-reference.md with correct browser/aiTools/versionManagers/schemaVersion shapes

Closes #90, #91, #92, #93, #94

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(implement): mark 011-wizard-bug-fixes implemented

Closes: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(analyze): add consistency report for 011-wizard-bug-fixes

- Mark T003–T026 as complete [X] in tasks.md
- Add deferral notes to T006, T007, T014
- Correct T017 test file reference (config-summary.test.ts)
- Note T018/T019 as pre-existing-no-change
- Fix plan.md R6 browser field shape to { selected: string[], default: string|null }
- Advance spec.md status from Implemented → Analyzed

Refs: #90, #91, #92, #93, #94

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(wizard): clarify extractStepValues configurations overlap comment

Steps 6 and 7 both carry 'configurations' in their history frames
because the Tools step and Editor Config step both write to that field.
Each frame must contain it so back-nav to either step restores the
correct partial state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(step-nav): remove orphaned isInputFocused and onAtFirstStep props

StepNav is not imported by any step component. Focus-safe back-nav for
text inputs is handled per-component via GateInput's !editing guard in
contexts.tsx. The first-step hint is wired at the wizard level via its
own useInput call — not through StepNav.

Remove the two unused props, simplify useInput back to always-active,
and document the per-component back-nav pattern in the file header.
Update wizard-navigation tests to drop the onAtFirstStep render test.

Closes #113

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(analyze): update tasks.md to reflect StepNav prop cleanup (T003b)

Mark T003/T004 as superseded by post-analysis refactor.
Add T003b tracking the isInputFocused/onAtFirstStep removal (closes #113).
Update T008 description to match actual test coverage after removal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): resolve 4 manual-test bugs

- Fix blank OS in config summary by initializing wizard config state
  with detectOS() so config.os is always populated from step 0
- Clear checkpoint when wizard fully completes (advance past LAST_STEP)
  so re-running tilde after a completed session no longer shows the
  stale 'Resume from step 12' prompt
- Remove now-redundant clearCheckpoint() from step-11 onComplete since
  the advance() function handles cleanup at the final step boundary
- Auto-select pre-installed AI tools on first visit to AI Tools step
  (savedNames absent → selected defaults to entry.installed instead of false)

Closes #100

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): cast detectOS() to 'macos' to satisfy TildeConfig type

TildeConfig.os is z.literal('macos') — OS type ('macos'|'linux'|'windows')
is wider. The cast is safe since assertMacOS() guards the wizard entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): resolve resume UX, Finish hang, and config discovery

- app.tsx: Wizard onComplete now calls setDone(true) and renders a
  completion screen; removes stale 'process.exit is handled by
  ConfigExportStep' comment that was wrong
- wizard.tsx: resume prompt shows 'Edit configuration' when lastCompletedStep
  >= LAST_STEP (fully completed); shows 'Resume from step N' otherwise
- wizard.tsx: fix OS spread order so checkpoint partialConfig.os=undefined
  can't clobber the detectOS() default (initialConfig spread first, os last)
- apply.tsx: call onComplete() after 1.5s when Apply succeeds so wizard
  completes cleanly instead of hanging on the done screen
- writer.ts: writeConfig always writes a canonical copy to
  ~/.tilde/tilde.config.json so discoverConfig() finds the config
  regardless of where the dotfiles repo lives; dotfilesRepo param is
  now optional (falls back to ~/.tilde/)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(wizard): add Edit/Start Over options to config-first mode

After completing the wizard and re-running tilde, the config-first
mode now shows three choices instead of one:
  - Apply this configuration (existing)
  - Edit configuration  → launches ReconfigureMode with existing config
  - Start over (run wizard) → launches fresh Wizard

app.tsx gains a configEditMode state ('apply' | 'edit' | 'start-over')
to select between the three rendering branches when a config is found.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(config-summary): show config file location in summary

Added optional configPath prop to ConfigSummary component, rendered
as a dimmed 'Config: <path>' row at the bottom of the summary box.

config-first mode passes the loaded configPath so users can see exactly
which file was used.  apply.tsx omits it (path not yet known before
apply runs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(011): update constitution, spec, tasks, and site docs for post-PR fixes

Constitution (2.4.0 → 2.4.1 PATCH):
- Schema version in Config export step: 1.5 → 1.6
- Entry Modes table: Config-first now documents Apply/Edit/Start Over/Cancel
- Principle IV: config-first confirmation MUST offer all four options
- Sync impact report updated

specs/011-wizard-bug-fixes:
- spec.md: added US8 (config-first Edit/Start Over), US9 (OS/AI/apply
  regressions); added FR-012–FR-018 and SC-008–SC-010
- tasks.md: Phase 10 T027 marked done; added Phase 11 with T028–T035
  covering all post-PR manual test bug fixes

site/docs/getting-started.md:
- Fixed config detection search order (cwd → git-root → ~/.tilde/)
- Added 'Re-running tilde' section documenting Apply/Edit/Start Over/Cancel
- Updated 'Updating your config' to lead with Edit configuration as primary
  flow, with tilde update as the targeted alternative

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tests): guard canonical config write in contract tests (H1)

- afterEach in config-schema.test.ts now deletes ~/.tilde/tilde.config.json
  after each test so contract runs no longer silently overwrite the
  developer's real config (review finding H1)
- apply.tsx: store setTimeout handle in completeTimerRef and clear on
  unmount to prevent stale-closure call if component unmounts early (M1)
- wizard.tsx: remove redundant currentStep dep from skip useCallback —
  skip only delegates to advance which already captures currentStep (M2)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(apply): pass explicit undefined to useRef to satisfy TS strict types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
